$schema.description: A URL to the JSON Schema for this object.

location.description: Where the error occurred, e.g. 'body.items[3].tags' or 'path.thing-id'

value.description: The value at the given location

$schema.description: A URL to the JSON Schema for this object.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.
examples: Property foo is required but is missing.

errors.description: Optional list of individual error details

instance.description: A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem.

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem type. This value should not change between occurrences of the error.

type.description: A URI reference to human-readable documentation for the error.

last_error.description: Most recent poll error, empty when healthy

last_seen_unix_ns.description: Nanoseconds since epoch of the last successful poll, 0 before the first success

name.description: Peer cluster name (manifest-derived once seen, else the operator-supplied label)

region.description: Free-form locality from the peer's manifest entry (e.g. 'eu-west-3') ; empty if never seen

status.description: 'live' | 'stale' | 'unreachable' — derived from LastSeen + LastError + StaleTTL on each poll

url.description: Peer /cluster-info URL — the poller targets this on every tick

weight.description: Placement bias from the peer's manifest entry ; 0 means default (100)

$schema.description: A URL to the JSON Schema for this object.
A URL to the JSON Schema for this object.

get.description: Returns the most recent N events from the audit JSONL file (newest first). Without --audit-log-path the endpoint returns enabled=false + an empty list, so the dashboard can show a friendly "audit log not enabled" panel instead of 503.

parameters.description: How many recent events to return
parameters.name: limit

schema.description: How many recent events to return

get.summary: Tail the recent audit log entries (cluster-admin)

post.summary: Register an availability zone (cluster-admin)

delete.description: Cascade-deletes every rack and host whose `az` column matches the AZ code. Use with care.
delete.summary: Delete an availability zone + cascade racks/hosts (cluster-admin)

delete.description: Idempotent — deleting a missing backup is a no-op. The URL is the same opaque addressing key returned by list-volume-backups ; the SPA passes it back verbatim.
delete.summary: Delete one backup from its target store

get.description: Walks the target's metadata sidecars and returns one row per backup. Filtered server-side by project (via the caller's visible projects). Optional ?volume_uuid= further narrows to one origin volume.
get.summary: List backups at a target store

post.description: Streams the snapshot's bytes to the target URL through weft-block. Encryption + incremental chains are honoured by the daemon when configured. Block-backend volumes only — file parents reject server-side with a clear error.
post.summary: Ship a snapshot to a backup target

post.description: Creates a fresh block-backend volume in the requested project and populates it from the backup. Size is discovered from the backup's sidecar metadata — the operator doesn't have to specify it. The source backup is untouched.
post.summary: Restore a backup into a new block volume
Create an object-storage bucket

delete.summary: Delete a bucket (cascades the attached policy)

parameters.description: Object key (full path within the bucket)
parameters.name: key

schema.description: Object key (full path within the bucket)

parameters.description: Object key (full path within the bucket)
parameters.name: key

schema.description: Object key (full path within the bucket)

get.summary: Get one bucket object (s3:GetObject gate)

parameters.description: Filter to entries under this prefix
parameters.name: prefix

schema.description: Filter to entries under this prefix

get.summary: List bucket objects (s3:ListBucket gate)

post.summary: Upload one or more bucket objects (s3:PutObject gate on the destination prefix)

get.summary: Get a bucket's IAM policy (empty {Statements:[]} when none)

put.description: An empty statement list clears the policy back to the default-allow state. One bad statement rejects the whole submission so the editor and server don't go out of sync.

post.summary: Create a DNS record

put.description: Editable fields : name, type, value, ttl. Zone and source are immutable from this endpoint.
put.summary: Update a DNS record (mock-friendly ; live wiring TBD)

parameters.description: Override the session project (defaults to platform)
parameters.name: project

schema.description: Override the session project (defaults to platform)

post.summary: Create a DNS zone

put.description: Editable fields : name, role (primary/secondary/forward), ttl_default, backend, push_target.
put.summary: Update a DNS zone (mock-friendly ; live wiring TBD)

get.description: Surfaces the same rows the operator sees from `weft federation list`. Status is one of 'live' (recent successful poll), 'stale' (last poll older than the stale TTL, default 5 minutes) or 'unreachable' (no successful poll on record or current poll failed).
get.summary: List federation peers with last-seen + status

get.description: Cluster-wide compute envelope catalogue. Read-open on both listeners since the CreateVMModal picker needs it.
get.summary: List the cluster's compute flavors
Get one flavor by name

post.summary: Allocate a floating IP (live-only)

get.summary: Get editable metadata for one floating-ip

put.summary: Replace editable metadata for one floating-ip (admin)

post.summary: Map a floating IP to a target
Unmap a floating IP

get.summary: Liveness probe

post.summary: Register a host (cluster-admin)

delete.description: Hosts carry microVMs. The dashboard SHOULD block deletion when the count > 0 ; the API doesn't enforce that yet — it's idempotent.

post.summary: Create a load balancer

put.summary: Atomically replace a load balancer's backend list

get.description: Returns 401 with a login URL when there's no session so the SPA's api.ts can trigger the OIDC redirect uniformly.
get.summary: Current session : user + role flags + reachable scopes

post.description: A microVM is the combination of a flavor (sizing envelope), an image, and a scheduling policy ; cpu/ram/disk are not independent fields — they're resolved from the flavor catalogue server-side. Ingress (floating-ip / load-balancer) is best-effort post-create ; failures show up as `warnings` rather than a hard error. First-boot provisioning stamps weft.boot/* properties read by the in-guest weft-microvm-agent (pull model).
post.summary: Create a VM (flavor + image + scheduling)

get.summary: List groups authorized to access one microVM

post.summary: Authorize a (tenant, group) pair on this microVM (admin)

delete.summary: Remove a (tenant, group) authorization from this microVM (admin) — idempotent

get.description: Union of explicit per-VM assignments AND keys whose owner is a member of any authorized group. Same shape as the per-VM SSH-keys list.
get.summary: Derived SSH key set this microVM will see

parameters.description: Project namespace (defaults to the session project)
parameters.name: project

schema.description: Project namespace (defaults to the session project)

get.summary: List the VM's assigned SSH keys (resolved against the catalogue)

post.description: Idempotent — re-assigning an existing name is a no-op. The catalogue name must already exist ; unknown names are rejected with a 400.
post.summary: Assign a catalogue SSH key to the VM

put.description: Replace-set semantics. Unknown names cause the whole call to fail (no partial saves) so the operator never sees a half-applied state.
put.summary: Replace the VM's assigned SSH-key set

delete.summary: Remove a key assignment from the VM

get.description: ?tail caps the response to the last N bytes (default 65536). A VM with a giant console.log doesn't blow up the SPA on first open.

parameters.description: Cap the response to the last N bytes (0 = default 65536)
parameters.name: tail

schema.description: Cap the response to the last N bytes (0 = default 65536)

get.summary: Read the VM's serial console log

parameters.description: Project namespace (defaults to the session project)
parameters.name: project

schema.description: Project namespace (defaults to the session project)

get.summary: List per-VM application-level properties

parameters.description: Project namespace (defaults to the session project)
parameters.name: project

schema.description: Project namespace (defaults to the session project)

post.summary: Create or replace a per-VM property (upsert)

post.description: Acceptance-style endpoint — returns 202 with state=starting ; poll status for the actual transition.
post.summary: Start a stopped VM

get.summary: Read a VM's current status

post.summary: Stop a running VM

get.summary: Read a VM's boot/lifecycle event timings

parameters.description: Project namespace (defaults to the session project)
parameters.name: project

schema.description: Project namespace (defaults to the session project)

get.summary: List the VM's UEFI NVRAM variables

post.description: Empty namespace defaults to the EFI Global Variable GUID so the common case (BootOrder, SecureBoot, …) doesn't require typing the GUID. value_hex has whitespace stripped before validation.
post.summary: Create or replace a UEFI variable (upsert on (namespace, name))

get.description: Admin-only — exposes host placement for every node. Mounted on the admin listener only ; the user listener returns 404 so a stale SPA build never accidentally reveals which host runs a VM.
get.summary: Mesh graph of overlay networks + attached workloads (admin)

post.summary: Create a network (live-only)

put.description: Updates the human-readable name. Attached VMs keep referencing by uuid.

parameters.description: Network identifier (name today ; uuid once live wiring lands)
parameters.name: key

schema.description: Network identifier (name today ; uuid once live wiring lands)

get.summary: Get the editable metadata layer for one network

put.description: Description + DNS servers. UpdatedAt / UpdatedBy stamped server-side. Live wiring forwards DNS to SetNetworkDNS.
put.summary: Replace the editable metadata for one network (admin)

get.summary: List subnets attached to one network

post.description: Upsert by uuid (when supplied) or by name. UpdatedAt / UpdatedBy stamped server-side.
post.summary: Create or update a subnet inside a network (admin)

get.summary: List installable plugins (*-as-a-service modules)

get.description: Each catalogue entry advertises the inputs the operator must provide on install — name, kind, type (string|number|bool|secret), required flag, default. The SPA renders a form from this schema and POSTs back to /api/plugins/install.
get.summary: List installable plugin definitions with their input schema

post.description: Body carries the catalogue plugin name, the target project, and a map of input values. The agent provisions the underlying resources (database / cache / topic set / …) and returns the instance UUID the operator can reference in future `weft plugin` commands. When the agent is wired the call drives `pluginstore.Manager.Install` ; otherwise the request fails with 400.
post.summary: Install a plugin with form inputs — returns the new instance UUID

get.description: Surfaces each instance the operator has provisioned via `weft plugin install` (or via the dashboard's plugin install drawer). Includes the bound VMs the instance manages, the install timestamp, and a status flag.
get.summary: List installed plugin instances

post.summary: Temporarily disable an installed plugin (cluster-admin) — hides its sidebar entry without uninstalling
Enable an installed plugin (cluster-admin)
Install a plugin (cluster-admin) — exposes its resources in the sidebar

post.description: Resource rows belonging to the uninstalled plugin remain in the mock store ; reinstalling restores visibility instantly. Live wiring would drain workloads first.
post.summary: Uninstall a plugin (cluster-admin) — its resources disappear from the sidebar

get.summary: Get a project's quota view

put.description: Validated against the tenant cap : sum(other projects) + new ≤ cap.

post.description: The role string is webui-side metadata for now (weft-agent has membership but no per-project role enum yet) ; we mirror it on the store and also call AddProjectMember so weft-agent sees the membership.
post.summary: Grant a per-project role to a user (tenant admin)

get.description: When the session carries a tenant scope, returns the tenant's quotas mapped to the 12 cluster-overview dimensions. Otherwise falls back to the static cluster-wide demo numbers.
get.summary: Scope-aware quota overview

post.summary: Register a rack (cluster-admin)

delete.summary: Delete a rack + cascade its hosts (cluster-admin)

get.description: Returns 200 + ok:true when the server can serve traffic ; returns 503 + ok:false when a wired persistence path isn't writable. Used by k8s readinessProbe / load-balancer health to take a node out of rotation if its state disk failed.
get.summary: Readiness probe — surfaces controller mode + state-file writability
List remote OCI registries (proxy / replica configuration)

post.description: Insert-or-update by Name. The LastSync field is owned by the sync engine — caller-supplied values are ignored.
post.summary: Create or update a remote registry (cluster-admin)

get.summary: Get one remote registry by name

get.description: Substring match on repository name. Live wiring routes to the remote's catalog API ; the mock returns canned results against a fixed corpus per remote.

parameters.description: Repository substring to search for. Empty returns a curated 'featured' subset.
parameters.name: q

schema.description: Repository substring to search for. Empty returns a curated 'featured' subset.

get.summary: Search a remote OCI registry for matching images

post.description: Accepts a multipart form with type / repository / tag / registry / arch / file. Persists a registry row so the dashboard round-trips.
post.summary: Register an OCI artifact (mock — records the row, no real push)

get.description: Scope-filtered : the user listener excludes admin-only entries (hosts / users / tenants) so a stale SPA never even sees they exist.
get.summary: List the visible resource catalogue (sidebar)

get.description: Polymorphic across the catalogue. Live-first for resources weft-agent ships ; mem-fallback otherwise. ?limit=N&page_token=... ; the cursor is opaque (base64 offset today, real keyset cursor once the upstream paginates).

parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a previous page
parameters.name: page_token

schema.description: Opaque cursor from a previous page

get.summary: Paginated row listing for one resource kind

parameters.description: Override the session project (defaults to platform)
parameters.name: project

schema.description: Override the session project (defaults to platform)

post.summary: Create a router (weft-network controller)

get.summary: Get editable metadata for one router

put.summary: Replace editable metadata for one router (admin)

post.summary: Create a scheduling rule (live-first ; mem fallback on Unimplemented)

get.summary: Get editable metadata for one scheduling-rule

put.summary: Replace editable metadata for one scheduling-rule (admin)

get.description: Filters the microvms catalogue by the row's `scheduling_rule` field (set when a rule expands to N replicas).
get.summary: List the microVMs deployed under one scheduling rule

patch.description: Partial-update : every field is optional. Empty-string on AZ/Rack/Host clears the axis. The live weft-network has no Update RPC yet, so this targets the in-memory store ; the SPA can mutate immediately and the operator sees the refresh.
patch.summary: Update placement / count / selector of a scheduling rule

get.description: The body is the heavy payload ; the CreateVMModal picker uses this listing then lazy-loads bodies via GET /api/scripts/{name}.
get.summary: List provisioning scripts (metadata only — body omitted)

post.description: UpdatedAt + UpdatedBy are server-stamped from the auth context ; the wire can't lie about provenance.
post.summary: Create or update a script (admin)

delete.description: Missing scripts return 200 with the requested name in 'deleted', so a retried client doesn't see a confusing 404.

get.summary: Get one script (body included)

post.summary: Create a security group (live-only)

put.description: Updates name and/or description in the seed catalogue. Live wiring will route to RenameSecurityGroup + SetSecurityGroupDescription once exposed via huma.

get.description: Live-first ; on Unimplemented OR no daemon, falls back to the mock 'security-rules' resource (matched by group name) so the dashboard stays explorable before weft-agent's SG-rule embedding lands.
get.summary: Get a security group's rule list

put.description: Live-first ; falls back to an in-memory mock store (seeded from the static security-rules table on first read) when no live agent is wired, so the SecurityPage edit affordance works through staged rollouts.
put.summary: Atomically replace a security group's rules

post.description: POSIX (RWX) face of CubeFS. Live-first via weft-agent ; falls back to the mock store on Unimplemented so the dashboard stays useful before the daemon catches up with CreateShare.
post.summary: Create a share (tenant admin)

put.description: Grows capacity ; shrinking is not supported (returns 400). The CubeFS volume owns physical capacity — this updates the metadata that drives mount-time enforcement. ReadOnly toggles re-fan to mounting VMs on the next reconcile.
put.summary: Resize a share / toggle read-only (tenant admin)

parameters.description: Object key (full path within the bucket)
parameters.name: key

schema.description: Object key (full path within the bucket)

get.summary: Get one share object's metadata + preview

parameters.description: Filter to entries under this prefix
parameters.name: prefix

schema.description: Filter to entries under this prefix

get.summary: List the share's objects under ?prefix

post.summary: Upload one or more objects into a share

delete.summary: Delete a snapshot (does not touch the parent volume)

post.description: Clones the snapshot's contents into a fresh volume. The new volume lands in the same project as the snapshot — there's no cross-project restore.
post.summary: Restore a snapshot into a new volume

post.description: Rolls the parent volume's contents back to the snapshot's state. Only supported on block-backend volumes (file parents reject with 502 — the agent surfaces a clear 'block-only' error message). The volume should be detached first ; the agent enforces this at the driver layer.
post.summary: Revert the parent volume to this snapshot (block-backend only)

get.description: Cluster-wide named SSH keys. Operators push their own keys here ; per-VM key assignments reference these by name.
get.summary: List the SSH-key catalogue

post.description: PublicKey is parsed server-side ; the algorithm whitelist is closed (ssh-ed25519, ssh-rsa, ssh-dss, ecdsa-sha2-nistp{256,384,521}). The fingerprint is computed from the decoded blob and overwrites whatever the client sent.
post.summary: Create or update an SSH key (tenant-admin or cluster-admin)

post.description: Hits the provider's .keys endpoint (github/gitlab/forgejo) and stores every key not already present (dedup by fingerprint). Returns a summary of added/skipped counts.
post.summary: Bulk-import an upstream account's SSH keys (tenant-admin or cluster-admin)

delete.summary: Delete an SSH key (tenant-admin or cluster-admin) — idempotent

get.summary: Get one SSH key by name

get.description: One item per visible catalogue entry. When the session carries a tenant + project, counts narrow to that scope (mock + live agreed semantics).
get.summary: Per-resource row counts narrowed by the session scope

post.description: Live-first via weft-agent ; falls back to the mock store on Unimplemented so the affordance keeps working before the daemon catches up.
post.summary: Create a tenant (cluster admin)

get.description: Non-members get 404 (don't-acknowledge). The response annotates the caller's effective role so the SPA knows which affordances to render — saves a round-trip.
get.summary: Tenant detail (projects + members + groups + roles)

post.summary: Elect a tenant admin (cluster admin)
Add a member to a tenant (tenant admin)
Add a project to a tenant (tenant admin)

get.summary: Get a tenant's quota view (cap + allocated + remaining)

put.description: Rejects a cap below current allocation so tenant admins don't end up with negative remaining.

get.description: Aggregates rows across the microvm + volume registries, keyed on the tenant a project belongs to. Quotas live on the tenant ; usage is computed here so the dashboard renders bars against the cap without a second round-trip. Non-members get 404 (don't-acknowledge).
get.summary: Live usage roll-up for one tenant (VMs / CPU cores / storage GiB)

post.summary: Create a volume (live-only)

put.description: Updates the human-readable name. Attached VMs keep referencing the volume by uuid ; this is the dashboard label.

parameters.description: Current volume identifier (name today ; uuid once live wiring lands)
parameters.name: key

schema.description: Current volume identifier (name today ; uuid once live wiring lands)

parameters.description: Volume identifier (name today ; uuid once live wiring lands)
parameters.name: key

schema.description: Volume identifier (name today ; uuid once live wiring lands)

get.summary: Get the editable metadata layer for one volume

put.description: Free-form description + suggested mount-point + filesystem hint. UpdatedAt / UpdatedBy are stamped server-side.
put.summary: Replace the editable metadata for one volume (admin)

parameters.description: Volume identifier (name today ; uuid once live wiring lands)
parameters.name: key

schema.description: Volume identifier (name today ; uuid once live wiring lands)

get.summary: List the property bag attached to a volume

post.description: Inserts or updates by Key. The orchestration layer reads these to make placement / lifecycle decisions.
post.summary: Upsert a property on a volume (admin)

delete.summary: Delete one property on a volume (admin) — idempotent

post.description: weft-agent keys on VM UUID, not name ; the caller looks up the VM UUID from VMStatus or ListVMs before calling.
post.summary: Attach a volume to a VM (live-only)
Detach a volume from its current VM (live-only)

get.description: Returns every snapshot taken on the given parent volume, oldest-first by creation time. Empty list when the volume has none. Live-only ; 503 when no weft daemon is wired.
get.summary: List snapshots of a volume

post.description: Freezes the volume's current state under the given name. File-backed parents do a reflink CoW clone ; block-backed parents create a controller-side snapshot through weft-block. The snapshot name must be unique within the parent volume.
post.summary: Snapshot a volume
